title: Disposable multi-instance demo stacks
acceptance: M3-D5: one demo stack (postgres+redis) isolated alongside the dev anthropos stack — up/status/down, dev untouched. Verified live on a 16 GB box.

hardening.scope: the post-close extended-work surface (full injected stack + deployment/injection)
bugs_fixed_inline: apply-authn.sh: set -e+pipefail abort-before-guard on a no-match colony grep (+ || true)
migrate-demo.sh: SC2015 A && ok || warn -> if/then/else
hardening.prod_refactors: extracted newServer() in both fake servers + build_lines() in gen_injected_override.py (testability only)
hardening.gates: deploy alignment gate held 100%/100% (7/7); clerkenstein all 13 pkgs green -race; flake gate 3/3
hardening.finding_to_M4: the demo identity is user_clerkenstein / demo@anthropos.test / org_clerkenstein (DefaultDemoUser); deployrun/expressrun use a user_2clerkenstein FIXTURE only — M4 seed must use the real DefaultDemoUser identity

decisions.M3-D3: clone at latest release tag, not main
decisions.M3-D4: tooling home = own gitignored anthropos-demo/demo-stacks repo; skills+guide in rosetta
decisions.M3-D5: acceptance adjusted to 1 stack alongside dev (16 GB host can't fit 2 full stacks)

injection_status: FULL STACK PROVEN. /demo-up brought up a 13-container demo with all 5 Clerk-consuming services injected (disarmed colony) + fake FAPI/BAPI; the demo backend accepts a Clerkenstein token live (403 not 401), real Clerk never used. /demo-down tears it all down, dev stack untouched. Remaining for a fully FUNCTIONAL demo (not Clerk): migrations/seeding (M4), browser login, BAPI HTTPS cert.
carried_forward: M3-CF1 RESOLVED (running-app deployment proven). Optional remaining: verify the clerk-backend in-Docker cert/redirect + the webhook POST live (the load-bearing authn path is proven). Recipe: demo-stacks/inject/DEPLOYMENT-PROOF.md.
dev_stack_safety: every op -p demo-N scoped; down hard-refuses the dev project; verified the dev stack (12 containers) stayed intact across demo-1's lifecycle AND the failed app-bring-up attempt
resource_note: RAM was never a blocker — the whole dev stack uses ~0.9 GB (measured), not the 10-12 GB doc estimate. The full app built + ran fine alongside the dev stack.
skills_proven: /demo-up (up-injected.sh — full Clerk-free stack) + /demo-down (full teardown) demonstrated end-to-end live
